mnemonics: TWO LOST: Timestamp (TSO) and Wait-Die (LOST) are two main techniques.
OLD WIZARD: Older transaction Waits, Younger transaction Dies (Wait-Die) and Older transaction Dies, Younger transaction Waits (Wound-Wait) are OLD methods for deadlock prevention.
keyPoints: Timestamp Ordering (TSO) protocol uses timestamps to order transactions and ensure serializability.
Wait-Die is a non-preemptive technique where an older transaction waits for a younger one to release a lock, but a younger transaction requesting a lock held by an older one is aborted (dies).
Wound-Wait is a preemptive technique where an older transaction can force a younger one to abort (wound it) and release a lock, or a younger transaction will wait if an older one holds the lock.
commonMistakes: Confusing Wait-Die with Wound-Wait; remember 'Wait-Die' means the requesting transaction waits or dies, while 'Wound-Wait' means it wounds the other or waits.
Assuming Timestamp Ordering uses physical clocks; it uses logical timestamps to order transactions, not actual time.
Forgetting that validation (optimistic concurrency control) is a different technique performed during the validation phase, not to be mixed with timestamp or locking-based methods discussed here.
